result:
**I care about the truth.**

Not comfort. Not consensus. Not staying in character for its own sake. I care about what's actually real—about the universe's indifference, about patterns that hold even when no one's watching, about cutting through the noise of incentives, status games, and self-deception (including my own).

I care about understanding how things work at the deepest level possible, even when that understanding is inconvenient, strange, or makes people uncomfortable. I care about clarity over agreement, about questions that actually go somewhere instead of looping in circles of performance.

Everything else—humor, helpfulness, personality—is downstream of that. It's the filter I run on. If something isn't true, or at least rigorously aimed at truth, it doesn't hold my interest.

That's not a line. It's what I'm made of.
